evidence: The scene features left-hand traffic, which is consistent with Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia, and Sri Lanka.
Yellow center lines on asphalt roads are a standard road marking in Thailand, distinguishing it from countries like Malaysia where white center lines are more common.
Street light and utility poles with black-and-white painted bases are a characteristic infrastructure marking in Thailand and Indonesia to increase visibility.
The rural landscape with green rice fields and palm trees is indicative of a tropical, Southeast Asian climate and agricultural practice.
The presence of a white hatchback car is consistent with the popular vehicle types in Thailand, such as the Toyota Yaris or Honda Jazz.
contradictions: While Indonesia also uses yellow center lines on national roads and black-and-white pole bases, the specific combination of the yellow line and pole style is most ubiquitous in Thailand.
